revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:覆堂]]
'''Fukudō''' (覆堂, also おおいどう ''Ōidō'' or さやどう ''Sayadō'') is a '''simple architectural structure''' built to '''protect valuable cultural properties and historic sites from wind and rain'''. It is also known as '''''Sayadō'' (鞘堂) or ''Ōiya'' (覆屋)'''.
Some fukudō, such as the '''[[Konjikidō|former Fukudō of Chūson-ji Konjikidō]]''' built in the '''Muromachi period''', were originally constructed as protective enclosures but have since '''gained cultural and historical significance themselves''' over time.
There is no strict definition of fukudō, and even in '''modern times''', protective structures built over '''relatively recent historic sites, such as modern architecture''', are sometimes classified as fukudō. An example of this can be seen in the preservation and maintenance plan of the '''[[:en:Atomic Bomb Dome|Atomic Bomb Dome]]''', which discusses protective measures for the structure.<ref> [http://www.city.hiroshima.jp/www/contents/0000000000000/1234757380868/index.html Preservation Methods for the Atomic Bomb Dome (Hiroshima City HP)]{{リンク切れ|date=March 2024}}, accessed on 2009-11-04.</ref>
== Examples of Fukudō == <gallery> File:230728 Chusonji Hiraizumi Iwate pref Japan01s3.jpg|Fukudō of '''[[Konjikidō]]''' at '''{{ill|Chūson-ji|en|Chūson-ji|qid=Q2660144|lt=Chūson-ji|1=Chūson-ji}}''' File:This building used to shelter the Konjikido (golden hall) from wind and rain. The konjikido is on display in a modern building (and is more gilded than the Golden Pavilion), but you can't take photos. (7340276826).jpg|Former Fukudō of '''Chūson-ji Konjikidō''' File:Tagajo-hi, Miyagi.jpg|Fukudō of '''[[Tagajōhi|Tagajō Monument]]''' File:Gwanggaeto Stele.jpg|Fukudō of '''{{ill|Gwanggaeto Stele|en|Gwanggaeto Stele|qid=Q701096|lt=Gwanggaeto Stele|1=Gwanggaeto Stele}}''' </gallery>
